name: Baked Apples and Chestnut Puree
recipeIngredient: Mint leaves & thin orange slices for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. To prepare fresh chestnuts, cook them covered in water for 20 minutes until expanded and lighter in color.
3. For canned or bottled chestnuts, heat the liquid they came in.
4. Cook dried chestnuts according to package directions, then drain and rinse under cold water.
5. Cut off the flat side of each chestnut with a sharp knife, scoop out the meat with a spoon.
6. In a food processor, combine the chestnut meat with soy milk, apple juice, 5 tbsp maple syrup, 1 tsp cinnamon, and 1/4 tsp nutmeg.
7. Fold in raisins or currants.
8. Peel apples and remove the core from the top, leaving the bottom intact.
9. Hollow out each apple, leaving a shell about half an inch thick. Brush the shells with lemon juice to prevent discoloration and sprinkle inside with a little cinnamon.
10. Chop the scooped-out apple pieces and mix them with the chestnut puree.
11. Fill each apple shell with the chestnut-apple stuffing.
12. Place the apples in a greased baking dish, pouring wine and remaining syrup around the base of the apples.
13. Add 3 tbsp margarine and 1 cinnamon stick to the pan.
14. Bake for 30-40 minutes, basting occasionally with the wine mixture.
15. Garnish with mint leaves and thin orange slices before serving.
